svg.text: Prim vs Kruskal — Which to Pick
Strategy grow tree from frontier accept globally cheapest non-cycle
Data structure priority queue sort + union-find
Time (sparse) O((V+E) log V) O(E log V)
Time (dense) O(V²) with array O(V² log V) ← Prim wins
Online edge stream poor natural (sort by weight)
At every step, the partial tree T forms a cut: T vs. (V \ T).
By the cut property, the lightest edge crossing this cut is in some MST.
Prim adds exactly that edge each step → maintains MST invariant.
key[v] = minimum weight of edges from in-tree vertices to v.
Each vertex pulled from the heap is "the lightest edge crossing the current cut."
Prim is the cut-property algorithm; Kruskal is the cycle-property algorithm.
Same MST family, dual perspectives — a cleaner intuition than memorizing both.
